title: trifolium longipes var. pedunculatum
intro: Trifolium longipes var. pedunculatum, commonly known as the long-stalked clover, is a perennial plant in the legume family that grows in the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties, with its leaves and flowers containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Research suggests that Trifolium longipes var. pedunculatum may have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through various methods, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
